card.intuition: Maintain a stack that is always sorted (increasing or decreasing) — when a new element would violate the order, pop elements until the stack is valid again, processing each popped element at that moment.
card.analogy: A stack of plates ordered by size — when you add a larger plate, you remove all smaller ones first. Each removed plate gets processed (its relationship to the new plate is now known).
card.time_complexity: O(n) — each element is pushed and popped at most once
when_to_use: Next Greater Element (NGE): first element to the right that is larger
Largest rectangle in a histogram — pop when a bar shorter than the top is encountered
Daily temperatures, stock span, trapping rainwater
Any problem asking: for each element, what is the nearest larger/smaller element to its left or right?
gotchas: Decide whether your stack is monotonically increasing (pop when new > top, gives previous smaller element on pop) or decreasing (pop when new < top, gives NGE on pop)
Store indices, not values, in the stack — you need the index to compute distances and to write results
After the main loop, elements still in the stack have no next greater element — handle them explicitly

exercise.prompt: Use a monotonic stack to find the Next Greater Element for each position. Return -1 if none exists.
exercise.starter_code:
def next_greater_element(nums):
    n      = len(nums)
    result = [-1] * n
    stack  = []          # monotonically decreasing stack of indices
    for i in range(n):
        # Pop indices whose element is smaller than nums[i]
        while stack and nums[stack[-1]] < nums[i]:
            idx          = stack.pop()
            result[idx]  = ___________   # nums[i] is the NGE for idx
        stack.append(i)
    return result
hints: When nums[i] is greater than the element at the top of the stack, nums[i] is the Next Greater Element for that top index
Pop the index, write nums[i] into result at that index, then continue popping while the condition holds
exercise.test_code:
assert next_greater_element([2,1,2])        == [  -1, 2,-1], '[2,1,2]'
assert next_greater_element([4,1,2])        == [  -1, 2,-1], '[4,1,2]'
assert next_greater_element([1,3,2,4])      == [3,4,4,-1],   '[1,3,2,4]'
assert next_greater_element([1,2,3,4])      == [2,3,4,-1],   'ascending'
assert next_greater_element([4,3,2,1])      == [-1,-1,-1,-1],'descending'
assert next_greater_element([1])            == [-1],          'single'
print('PASS')
